findings.title: Raw NUL byte makes the Go source invalid
findings.evidence: The added string literal contains an actual U+0000 character between `Hello` and `World`, rather than an escaped sequence. Go rejects NUL characters in source text before it can treat this as string data.
findings.suggested_comment: `PrintText` adds an actual NUL character inside `fmt.Println("Hello...World")`. Go source files cannot contain U+0000, so this will fail to compile; if the intent is to print a null byte, please encode it with an escape such as `"Hello\x00World"`.
findings.fix_hint: Replace the raw control character in the string literal with a Go escape sequence like `\x00` or `\u0000`.
